items.title: How Israel's Air Defenses Inspired Donald Trump's "Golden Dome"
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Israel's Iron Dome has been so successful in its war in Gaza, it inspired Donald Trump to want to build his own missile shield. Bloomberg's Marissa Newman explains some of the challenges the US faces in developing the "Golden Dome".
--------

items.title: Trump Tariffs: Should US Brace for Recession?
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Andrew Hale, Senior Policy Analyst for Trade Policy at the Heritage Foundation, shares his thoughts on President Trump's reciprocal tariffs announcement and states that if these tariffs are maintained the United States may get a recession and high inflation. Hale also discusses how he believes these tariffs could deter President Trump's agenda, create victories for the Democratic party, and affect Trump's MAGA base. Andrew Hale speaks with Kailey Leinz and Joe Mathieu on the late edition on Bloomberg's "Balance of Power."
--------

items.title: How YouTube Beat Disney to Become the "New King of All Media"
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>With fears of search advertising growth slowing and Alphabet actively working to diversify beyond it — while still defending its dominance — Cloud and YouTube have become critical drivers of near- to medium-term growth. Together, these segments now contribute over 30% of total revenue and are the fastest-growing scaled businesses within Alphabet.

According to new research from MoffettNathanson, YouTube is set to become the “New King of All Media.” As of 2024, YouTube was already the second-largest media company by revenue at $54.2 billion, trailing only Disney. However, this dynamic is expected to shift in 2025, when YouTube is poised to take the top spot — making it not only the leader in engagement but revenue as well. If YouTube was a standalone business, public comps suggest the business would be worth $475 to $550 billion, or about 30% of Alphabet’s current valuation.  

MoffettNathanson co-founder and senior research analyst Michael Nathanson explains how YouTube's dominance is impacting other streaming giants like Netflix and Disney, and how less powerful content providers are being forced to adapt. Michael speaks with Tom Keene and Paul Sweeney on Bloomberg Radio.

items.title: The 'Trump Effect' on Europe’s Wake-Up Call to Capital Markets
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>How has the Trump administration’s pro-growth mindset served as an alarm clock to Europe? Executive Chair of Santander Ana Botín describes how US economic policies and the war in Ukraine are drivers of European countries “lifting the brakes”, including in Germany. Former Vice Chair of the Federal Reserve Randal Quarles breaks down why there’s been a chasm between the US and EU’s GDPs in the last decade and what important things that the EU can do to accelerate growth.
--------

items.title: Why Trump Tariffs Put Lucrative Transatlantic Flights at Risk
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>Optimism about travel demand has been put to the test by US President Donald Trump's tariffs with airlines including Virgin Atlantic warning of a dropoff in demand due to consumer uncertainty. Bloomberg's Benedikt Kammel reports.

items.title: Bloomberg is the first to try on the Rolex Oyster Perpetual Land-Dweller #luxury #shorts
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content:
<article>How does @ROLEX design and execute a totally new watch? With the new Oyster Perpetual Land-Dweller, they let Bloomberg's Chris Rovzar in on the process.

The Land-Dweller features a modern design inspired by vintage Rolex models, with a 5 Hz movement for increased precision and durability, and is offered in 40mm and 36mm cases with various materials and prices ranging from $14,900 to $88,300. Read more: http://bloom.bg/3E5rFg2

#watches #luxury #rolex #rolexwatch</article>

items.title: The growing popularity of bluefin tuna #shorts #tuna #wallstreetweek
items.author: Bloomberg Television
items.content: <article>Coral Omakase sources tuna from Japan and the east coast of the US. Chef Franklin Becker says the bluefin population has come back from overfishing, and tells Dani Burger about a process called ikejime. Watch "Wall Street Week" tonight at 6 p.m. ET http://bloom.bg/3G8d83J</article>
